title: DUX v9.5 UserOutcome Object
description: OKR-style tracking object linking user scenarios to measurable business outcomes. User Outcomes inherit key signals from linked user flows, creating traceable relationships between behaviors and measurable outcomes.

object_type.description: Object type discriminator - always 'UserOutcome' for UserOutcome objects

outcome_statement.description: Who does what by how much to make progress toward our target result? When our user succeeds how will our solution have changed their behavior?

user_scenario.description: High-level scenario or context for this outcome

target_impact_when_achieved.description: Expected business or user impact when this outcome is achieved

priority.description: Business priority level for this outcome

reference_context.description: LLM-generated explanation of why this relationship exists

flow_ids.description: INHERITANCE SOURCE: Array of Flow IDs that contribute to achieving this outcome. When present, the key_signals field should inherit signals from the behaviors within these flows. This creates a traceable relationship between user flows, their behaviors, and the measurable signals that indicate outcome achievement.

reference_context.description: LLM-generated explanation of why this relationship exists

problem_ids.description: Array of Problem IDs that this outcome addresses

reference_context.description: LLM-generated explanation of why this relationship exists

result_ids.description: Array of Result IDs that contribute to this outcome

end_user.description: User personas or roles who experience this outcome

key_signals.description: INHERITED: Observable signals inherited from key behaviors in linked user flows (flow_ids). When a user_flow is referenced, these signals are automatically inherited from the behaviors within that flow. If no user_flow is linked, this field is optional and may be populated by ETL pipeline suggestions. Each signal should correspond to a measurable behavior or action that indicates progress toward this outcome.

acceptance_criteria.description: Clear, testable criteria that define successful achievement of this outcome

evidence_maturity.description: Progressive evidence maturity tier: 01_assumptive (inferred, no direct support), 02_anecdotal (1-2 qualitative signals), 03_early_signal (recurring signal, no quant), 04_balanced_signal (qualitative + quantitative), 05_triangulated (cross-method validation)

evidence.description: Array of Provenance IDs that support this outcome
